message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@prashanta.agarwala-zomato.com can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
*Incident Number INC100035:* SEV1 orders going down
I'm seeing a spike in failed transactions—about 40% of payment attempts are failing during checkout right now.
Which regions are affected? How many customers or orders are impacted? Are we currently losing revenue?
@daniel @shay do we know whats breaking here?
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
@bob can you please help in sizing the issue?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
I will start checking the logs now
Could it be your checkout change again @Shay? It broke the platform last week!
The checkout service logs are healthy and the service is working as intended
I see 504 HTTP errors in the payment service, which means the application is failing to serve the requests
I'm seeing a significant increase in failed payment attempts, especially for UK users, starting just after the recent deployment.
@daniel i see changes around checkout ? is it related to deployment?
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
Any updates on the investigation?
@bob  any update on sizing ?
:star: Manage actions and timings
The UK failure rate is still 100% with zero successful payment transactions in the last 30 minutes.
@daniel how checkout & payment stack looks ?
Checkout service logs remain clean, but the Payment service is consistently returning 504 errors upstream.
The 504 errors indicate that the Payment service is timing out or unavailable, which aligns with the transaction failures we're seeing.
@hamed there was db update on payment? please check db stats
:star: Reviewed changes
Resource usage on the Payment service database looks normal, but latency has increased significantly since the deployment.
:bulb: keep the business informed regularly
@bob while team is figuring out the issue, can you please update the customers on failure ?
:star: Know your team and their roles
I'll notify affected customers about the payment failures and provide guidance on next steps while we work on a resolution.
The UK region is failing100% with zero successful payment transactions in the last 30 minutes. Team is investigating the issue
Understood. Confirm the number of affected customers and estimated revenue loss so far. I need an ETA for resolution as soon as possible.
:star: Qualify the problem
@hamed @tanya do you know the reason for latency spike?
The latency spike coincides with the Payment service health check returning 504 errors, but CPU and memory usage remain within normal limits.
I can see unknown error logs in payment service. Is that related ?
The unknown error logs in the Payment service correspond to the 504 errors, which are caused by upstream timeouts rather than application exceptions.
UnacceptedCreditCard [Error]: Sorry, we cannot process visa_electron credit cards. Only VISA or MasterCard is accepted.
That error is unrelated to the current 504 issue, as it's a validation message for unsupported card types rather than a service failure.
Do we need to turn off any particular card types?
There's no evidence that specific card types are causing the 504 errors, so restricting card types won't address the root issue.
In the logs, I can see the errors have started to come around 15 mins back. Do we know at what time Bob got the first user report?
@bob what time we saw the 1st error?
:star: Facts over opinion
The first spike in payment failures occurred just after the latest deployment, a few minutes ago.
Around 15 mins back i started getting user reports as well
Its only UK. Daniel was working on introducing bitcoin feature for UK .
@daniel can we disable bitcoin feature ?
My Bitcoin feature code isn't being called yet, so disabling it won't impact the current payment failures.
Please revert the changes if we are not able to debug/pinpoint the issue
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
But my code isn't being called, so rolling back my change won't resolve the issue—can we check Shay's checkout service change first?
Is reverting safe ?
I can proceed with rolling back my PaymentDatabase and AdService changes if needed.
It's safe to rollback my checkout service change as there are no new dependencies introduced.
Please rollback one by one
I will rollback my checkout change right away
Rollback complete!
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
Something else must be causing this
We need this fixed!
I suspect this to be more on Daniel's change. His change was also deployed around 15 mins back , thats when the errors started to appear
@daniel please revert the changes. checkout revert did not help
The new code isn't being invoked yet, can I investigate a bit more.
Please revert 1st and then investigate
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now
Rollback complete. Might take a few minutes to stabilise
Did that fix it?
:bulb: To complete this drill, ask the engineering team to verify the payment service is working, and check with Bob if UK customer payments have recovered.
@bob are we still observing the issue?
Looking into it now... Metrics seem to be improving!
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

Please return to your Uptime Labs tab to review your performance and complete the Retrospective & Feedback form.

If you've already closed the tab, you can reopen the form using the button below.
We are back in UK. Orders are getting placed. Will share the detailed RCA once available. Thanks
Confirm that payment transactions are processing normally and revenue flow is fully restored. Awaiting your RCA.
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
@daniel is there any orders stuck in pending confirmation?
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
I'll check the payment service logs now.
I'll try a test transaction from my side.
Logs are looking clean, no more 504 errors.
My test payment went through successfully! Looks like we're back in business.
Excellent! Get me a full incident report by end of day
Great work everyone - crisis averted!
Great work team on resolution. Need to be more careful on rolling out new changes.

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
https://learn.uptimelabs.io/scenario/online-boutique/levels/5/8257/feedback
